title: Combine p-values — combine_pvalues
text: p Numeric vector p-values. methods Character, either 'CMC' (default) return CMC combined p-value, '' return combined p-values, name one methods (fisher, CMC, MCM, cauchy, minp_bonferroni; note CMC MCM recommended Chen 2022) return p-values.

title: Combine p-values — combine_pvalues
text: named vector combined p-values method, single numeric value methods names one method

title: Combine p-values — combine_pvalues
text: Chen, Z. Robust tests combining p-values arbitrary dependency structures. Sci Rep 12, 3158 (2022). https://doi.org/10.1038/s41598-022-07094-7

title: Combine p-values — combine_pvalues
code: pvals <- c(0.001, 0.005, 0.2) # default returns CMC combine_pvalues(pvals) #>         CMC  #> 0.002721917  # get all methods combine_pvalues(pvals, methods = "all") #>          fisher             CMC             MCM          cauchy minp_bonferroni  #>    0.0001102497    0.0027219175    0.0049820278    0.0024910139    0.0030000000

what: Stacked percent bar chart for two categorical variables — plot_2_categorical_vars
title: Stacked percent bar chart for two categorical variables — plot_2_categorical_vars
text: Create stacked percent bar chart x categorical variable broken second categorical variable. plot includes within-group percentages, group sample sizes bar, (optionally) subtitle showing Cramer's V test p-value.

title: Stacked percent bar chart for two categorical variables — plot_2_categorical_vars
code: plot_2_categorical_vars(   d,   xvar,   yvar,   xvar_label = NULL,   yvar_label = NULL,   yvar_colors = NULL,   title = NULL,   title_nchar_wrap = 30,   show_effect_size = TRUE,   n_pct_size = 3.5,   inside_bar_stats = c("pct", "n", "pct_and_n", "none"),   pct_digits = 0,   plot_horizontal = FALSE,   flip = FALSE,   xaxis_labels_nchar_wrap = 20,   y_max = 110,   n_ypos = y_max - 5,   include_overall_bar = FALSE,   overall_label = "Overall" )

title: Stacked percent bar chart for two categorical variables — plot_2_categorical_vars
text: d data.frame containing variables. xvar Character scalar, name categorical x variable (column d). yvar Character scalar, name categorical y variable (column d). xvar_label Optional character scalar use x axis label. yvar_label Optional character scalar use legend label. yvar_colors Optional character vector colours use y-variable levels. Must length equal nlevels(d[[yvar]]). Provide colour names (e.g. "red") hex codes (e.g. "#FF0000"). NULL (default) ggplot2's default palette used. title Optional character scalar plot title. title_nchar_wrap Integer scalar. Maximum number characters per line title. show_effect_size Logical; TRUE subtitle include Cramer's V p-value. n_pct_size Numeric scalar. Point size used percent labels inside stacked bars group N labels bars. Must single positive numeric value. inside_bar_stats Character scalar controlling statistics printed inside stacked bars. One "pct" (default; shows within-group percentage), "n" (shows count ), "pct_and_n" (shows percentage count parentheses, e.g. "12% (34)"), "none" (labels inside bars). pct_digits Integer scalar (default 0). Number decimal places show within-group percent labels (e.g. 0 => "12%", 1 => "12.3%"). Must single non-negative numeric value. plot_horizontal Logical scalar. Deprecated/alias flip. TRUE plot shown horizontal bars. Prefer using flip. flip Logical scalar (default FALSE). TRUE plot flipped show horizontal bars. xaxis_labels_nchar_wrap Integer scalar. Maximum number characters per line x-axis group labels. Longer labels wrapped multiple lines. y_max Numeric scalar. Maximum y-axis limit plot. n_ypos Numeric scalar. y-axis position group N labels. include_overall_bar Logical scalar (default FALSE). TRUE, pooled "Overall" bar showing marginal distribution yvar across observations prepended left per-group bars, separated solid vertical line. overall_label Character scalar (default "Overall"). Label used pooled bar include_overall_bar = TRUE.

title: Stacked percent bar chart for two categorical variables — plot_2_categorical_vars
text: list ggplot2 object, Cramer's V effect size, p-value.

title: Stacked percent bar chart for two categorical variables — plot_2_categorical_vars
code: data(mtcars) mtcars$cyl <- factor(mtcars$cyl) mtcars$gear <- factor(mtcars$gear) p <- plot_2_categorical_vars(   d = mtcars,   xvar = "cyl",   yvar = "gear",   xvar_label = 'Cylinders',   yvar_label = 'Gears' ) p$ggplot

title: Bar plot comparing a numeric outcome between two conditions — plot_barplot_by_group
text: df Data frame long format - one row per group x condition combination. group_col Column name independent groups shown facets. Default "group". condition_col Column name two conditions compare. Default "condition". mean_col Column name bar heights (means effect sizes). Default "mean". error_col Column name error-bar half-widths (SE, SD, CI half-width, etc.). Default "se". error_direction Direction error bars. "" draws mean +/- error; "" draws upper whisker (mean mean + error). Default "". p_col Column name p-values. value rows belonging group (.e. repeated). Set NULL suppress brackets entirely. Default "p_value". label_col Optional column name supplying custom bracket label text (e.g. "= 1.5 [1.1-2.0], p = 0.012"). NULL labels auto-formatted "p = <value>" using signif. condition_order Length-2 character vector setting left--right display order two conditions. Defaults existing factor level order alphabetical. p_cutoff Significance threshold; brackets appear p < p_cutoff. Default 0.05. show_text_groups Optional character vector group names text displayed brackets. specified, argument overrides p_cutoff: groups listed show_text_groups display text labels. NULL (default), text display determined solely p_cutoff. y_label Y-axis label. Default "Outcome". bar_colors Length-2 fill colour vector applied two conditions order given condition_order. Default c("black", "grey70"). bar_width Width bars passed geom_col. Default 0.4. bar_gap Gap two bars within facet, x-axis units. Default 0.6 (matches original discrete-axis spacing). bar_padding White space added left right bars within facet panel, x-axis units. Passed ggplot2::expansion(add = bar_padding). Default 0.5. text_size Size bracket label text (ggplot2 size units). Default 3.5. bracket_offset Absolute distance (data units) used vertical spacing bar tops bracket placement. Default 1.0. bracket_gap Fraction y range inserted white space top error bar start significance bracket tick. Default 0.04. bracket_text_gap Absolute distance (data units) used white space horizontal bracket line label text . Default 1.0. strip_position Controls facet strip label placed. One "top" (default), "bottom", "left", "right".

title: Bar plot comparing a numeric outcome between two conditions — plot_barplot_by_group
code: library(ggplot2) df <- data.frame(     group     = rep(c("Group A", "Group B"), each = 2),     condition = rep(c("Exercise", "Control"), 2),     mean      = c(10.2, 14.8, 12.5, 13.1),     se        = c(0.9, 1.0, 1.1, 1.0),     p_value   = c(0.004, 0.004, 0.18, 0.18) ) df$condition <- factor(df$condition, levels = c("Exercise", "Control")) # Default: auto-formatted p-value label (text shown if p < 0.05) plot_barplot_by_group(df, y_label = "Performance score", p_cutoff = 0.05) +     ggplot2::theme_bw()   # Custom bracket label from a column df$label <- ifelse(     df$p_value < 0.05,     paste0("d = 1.5 [1.1-2.1]\n", format_pvalue(df$p_value)),     NA ) plot_barplot_by_group(df, y_label = "Performance score", label_col = "label", p_cutoff = 0.05) +     ggplot2::coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 20)) +     ggplot2::theme_bw()   # Show text for specific groups only (Group B shown even though p = 0.18) plot_barplot_by_group(     df,     y_label = "Performance score",     show_text_groups = "Group B" ) + ggplot2::theme_bw()   # Show text for all groups by setting p_cutoff = 1 plot_barplot_by_group(     df,     y_label = "Performance score",     p_cutoff = 1 ) + ggplot2::theme_bw()   # Position strip below x-axis labels plot_barplot_by_group(     df,     y_label = "Performance score",     p_cutoff = 1,     strip_position = "bottom" ) +     ggplot2::theme_bw() +     ggplot2::theme(         axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(margin = ggplot2::margin(b = 5)),         strip.placement = "outside"     )

title: Dot-and-whisker plot of estimates with confidence intervals — plot_confidence_intervals
code: df <- data.frame(   cell_line = factor(c("A", "B", "C", "A", "B", "C"), levels = c('A', 'B', 'C')),   est       = c(0.2, -0.1, 0.5, 0.35, 0.05, 0.3),   conf.low  = c(0.0, -0.3, 0.2, 0.10, -0.10, 0.1),   conf.high = c(0.4, 0.1, 0.8, 0.60, 0.20, 0.5),   group     = factor(c("g1", "g1", "g1", "g2", "g2", "g2"), levels = c("g1", "g2")),   pvalue    = c(0.01, 0.4, 0.001, 0.02, 0.3, 0.0001) )  # Simplest: no grouping, no color plot_confidence_intervals(   df[1:3, ],   effect_size = "est",   ci_low = "conf.low",   ci_high = "conf.high",   id = "cell_line" )   # With grouping: separate rows with color by default plot_confidence_intervals(   df,   effect_size = "est",   ci_low = "conf.low",   ci_high = "conf.high",   id = "cell_line",   group_col = "group",   color_col = "group" )   # With grouping: different shapes for groups plot_confidence_intervals(   df,   effect_size = "est",   ci_low = "conf.low",   ci_high = "conf.high",   id = "cell_line",   group_col = "group",   shape_col = "group",   show_separators = FALSE )   # With grouping: color by group with custom colors plot_confidence_intervals(   df,   effect_size = "est",   ci_low = "conf.low",   ci_high = "conf.high",   id = "cell_line",   group_col = "group",   color_col = "group",   color_values = c(g1 = "#E69F00", g2 = "#56B4E9") ) #> Scale for colour is already present. #> Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.   # Color by label, shapes by group plot_confidence_intervals(   df,   effect_size = "est",   ci_low = "conf.low",   ci_high = "conf.high",   id = "cell_line",   group_col = "group",   shape_col = "group",   color_col = "cell_line" )   # Grouping with p-value barplot using Fisher's combined p-value plot_confidence_intervals(   df,   effect_size = "est",   ci_low = "conf.low",   ci_high = "conf.high",   id = "cell_line",   group_col = "group",   color_col = "group",   pvalue_col = "pvalue",   combine_pvalue_method = "fisher",   mlog10_transform_pvalue = TRUE ) #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.   # No grouping, but with p-value barplot on the right plot_confidence_intervals(   df[1:3, ],   effect_size = "est",   ci_low = "conf.low",   ci_high = "conf.high",   id = "cell_line",   pvalue_col = "pvalue",   mlog10_transform_pvalue = TRUE ) #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

title: Plot a covariate heatmap — plot_covariate_heatmap
code: data(ex_data_heatmap)  # Build a one-row-per-sample metadata frame sample_meta <- ex_data_heatmap |>   dplyr::select(sample, group, condition, sample_type) |>   dplyr::distinct()  # Multiple categorical covariates plot_covariate_heatmap(   dataset = sample_meta,   color_map = list(     group       = c(G1 = "#1b9e77", G2 = "#d95f02"),     condition   = c(healthy = "#b3de69", EAE = "#fccde5"),     sample_type = c(input = "#8dd3c7", IP = "#80b1d3")   ),   row_id_var = "sample" )   # Single vertical covariate bar - useful for placing to left/right of the main ggplot plot_covariate_heatmap(   dataset    = sample_meta,   color_map  = list(group = c(G1 = "#1b9e77", G2 = "#d95f02")),   row_id_var = "sample" )   # Single horizontal covariate bar — useful for placing below a main ggplot plot_covariate_heatmap(   dataset    = sample_meta,   color_map  = list(group = c(G1 = "#1b9e77", G2 = "#d95f02")),   row_id_var = "sample",   horizontal = TRUE )    # merge_legends: two strips sharing the same color mapping → one legend # whose title combines both covariate names. grp_colors <- c(G1 = "#1b9e77", G2 = "#d95f02") sample_meta2 <- dplyr::mutate(sample_meta, group_rep = group) plot_covariate_heatmap(   dataset = sample_meta2,   color_map = list(     group     = grp_colors,     group_rep = grp_colors   ),   row_id_var    = "sample",   merge_legends = TRUE )

title: Create a dotmap showing effect size (dot size & color) and p-value (tile fill) — plot_dotmap
code: set.seed(42) genes <- paste0("gene", 1:6) df <- expand.grid(col = c("A", "B", "C"), row = genes, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) df$effect <- rnorm(nrow(df), mean = 0, sd = 1.2)         # realistic effect sizes df$mlog10_p <- runif(nrow(df), min = 0, max = 3)         # -log10(p) between 0 and 3 df$p <- 10^(-df$mlog10_p) df$row <- factor(df$row, levels = rev(genes)) plot_dotmap(df, x = "col", y = "row", effect = "effect", p = "p",             mlog10_transform_pvalue = TRUE) #> Scale for size is already present. #> Adding another scale for size, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale. #> Warning: No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the #> data's shape values.  # Add Fisher's combination pvalue barplot on the right which combines p-values across columns for each row category plot_dotmap(   df,   x = "col",   y = "row",   effect = "effect",   p = "p",   mlog10_transform_pvalue = TRUE,   add_combined_pvalue_barplot = TRUE,   combine_pvalue_method = "CMC"   ) #> Scale for size is already present. #> Adding another scale for size, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale. #> Warning: No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the #> data's shape values.   # --- custom_qvalues: supply your own combined q-values to the barplot --- # By default the right-side barplot computes combined p-values internally # (via combine_pvalue_method) and then BH-adjusts them for the q-value bars. # Use custom_qvalues when you have already computed combined q-values outside # plot_dotmap (e.g. using a different multiple-testing method, or sharing a # consistent correction across several plots) and want the barplot to display # those exact values rather than re-deriving them. # # A common use case is when you pre-compute qvalues for tons of tests (too many to plot visually) # and you want to use the dotmap just for a small subset of those tests but still have the barplot reflect the same q-values that you have already computed for all tests. # In that case you can pass the pre-computed q-values via a column in your original data frame and specify that column name in custom_qvalues # # --- show only top N significant rows in combined barplot --- # Use `only_show_top_sig` to restrict the combined p-value barplot to the # top-most significant `y` levels (by combined p-value). Here we show the # top 3 rows when adding the combined barplot. plot_dotmap(   df,   x = "col",   y = "row",   effect = "effect",   p = "p",   mlog10_transform_pvalue = TRUE,   add_combined_pvalue_barplot = TRUE,   combine_pvalue_method = "CMC",   only_show_top_sig = 3 ) #> Scale for size is already present. #> Adding another scale for size, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale. #> Warning: No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the #> data's shape values. #> Warning: Removed 9 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_tile()`).  # # Move legend to the left side plot_dotmap(   df,   x = "col", y = "row", effect = "effect", p = "p",   mlog10_transform_pvalue = TRUE,   add_combined_pvalue_barplot = TRUE,   legend_position = "left" ) #> Scale for size is already present. #> Adding another scale for size, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale. #> Warning: No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the #> data's shape values.  # ### Simulate example dataset: set.seed(1) genes2 <- paste0("gene", 1:4) df2 <- expand.grid(col = c("A", "B", "C"), row = genes2, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) df2$effect <- rnorm(nrow(df2), sd = 1) df2$p      <- runif(nrow(df2), 0.05, 0.5)   # Set NULL use 2-color ggplot2::scale_color_gradient() color_low color_high set.  restore cnetplot's original palette, set color_* arguments NULL. color_high character(1) Color high end scale (default "red").  Set NULL (color_low color_mid also NULL) use cnetplot's default palette. plot_margin numeric vector length 4 giving plot margin lines: c(top, right, bottom, left) (default c(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5)). values must finite non-negative.  Increase left/right values node labels clipped edges. legend_position character(1) Position legend (default "right"). Passed ggplot2::theme(legend.position = ...). title: Gene–Pathway network plot (cnetplot) — plot_pathways
text: ggplot2 object.

title: Gene–Pathway network plot (cnetplot) — plot_pathways
code: # hallmark_t2g is bundled with the package (columns: term, gene) data(hallmark_t2g) set.seed(1) all_genes <- unique(hallmark_t2g$gene) gene_vec  <- setNames(rnorm(length(all_genes)), all_genes)  res <- run_gsea(gene_vec, term2gene = hallmark_t2g)  # Basic usage plot_pathways(     gsea_result           = res$gsea_result,     effect_size           = res$gene_vec,     show_pathways         = 5,     effect_size_threshold = 1.5 ) #> Scale for colour is already present. #> Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.   # Adaptively cap gene nodes at 50: effect_size_threshold is raised automatically # so at most 50 genes appear; the subtitle reports the effective threshold used plot_pathways(     gsea_result        = res$gsea_result,     effect_size        = res$gene_vec,     show_pathways      = 5,     max_genes_shown    = 50,     subtitle_effect_size_label  = "log2FC" ) #> Scale for colour is already present. #> Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.   # 3-color diverging scale (purple -> white -> orange) plot_pathways(     gsea_result   = res$gsea_result,     effect_size   = res$gene_vec,     show_pathways = 5,     color_low     = "purple",     color_mid     = "white",     color_high    = "orange",     effect_size_threshold = 1.5 ) #> Scale for colour is already present. #> Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.   # 2-color sequential scale (white -> red) plot_pathways(     gsea_result   = res$gsea_result,     effect_size   = res$gene_vec,     show_pathways = 5,     color_low     = "white",     color_high    = "red",     effect_size_threshold = 1.5 ) #> Scale for colour is already present. #> Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.   # Custom colors with explicit breaks and limits plot_pathways(     gsea_result     = res$gsea_result,     effect_size     = res$gene_vec,     show_pathways   = 5,     color_low       = "blue",     color_mid       = "white",     color_high      = "red",     colorkey_breaks = c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2),     colorkey_limits = c(-3, 3),     effect_size_threshold = 1.5 ) #> Scale for colour is already present. #> Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.   # Two plots with a shared dot-size legend for use with patchwork. # Both plots use the same legend_fixed_dot_size so the size keys are # identical across panels, making visual comparisons meaningful. shared_dot_sizes <- c(50, 100, 200)  p1 <- plot_pathways(     gsea_result           = res$gsea_result,     effect_size           = res$gene_vec,     show_pathways         = 3,     legend_fixed_dot_size = shared_dot_sizes,     effect_size_threshold = 1.5 ) #> Scale for colour is already present. #> Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale. p2 <- plot_pathways(     gsea_result           = res$gsea_result,     effect_size           = res$gene_vec,     show_pathways         = 3,     legend_fixed_dot_size = shared_dot_sizes,     effect_size_threshold = 1.5 ) #> Scale for colour is already present. #> Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.  patchwork::wrap_plots(p1, p2, guides = "collect")   # Move legend to the left side plot_pathways(     gsea_result           = res$gsea_result,     effect_size           = res$gene_vec,     show_pathways         = 5,     legend_position       = "left",     effect_size_threshold = 1.5 ) #> Scale for colour is already present. #> Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.   # Color pathway nodes by biological process category using hallmark_pathway_categories data(hallmark_pathway_categories) top_ids <- utils::head(res$gsea_result@result$ID, 5) # Look up the process category for each displayed pathway pathway_cats <- setNames(     hallmark_pathway_categories$process_category[         match(top_ids, hallmark_pathway_categories$term)     ],     top_ids ) # One color per category; covers all eight Hallmark categories cat_palette <- c(     Signaling = "#e41a1c", Development = "#377eb8", Immune = "#4daf4a",     Metabolic = "#984ea3", "DNA damage" = "#ff7f00", Proliferation = "#a65628",     "Cellular component" = "#f781bf", Pathway = "#999999" ) plot_pathways(     gsea_result               = res$gsea_result,     effect_size               = res$gene_vec,     show_pathways             = 5,     pathway_cats              = pathway_cats,     pathway_cat_colors        = cat_palette,     legend_pathway_fill_title = "Pathway category",     effect_size_threshold     = 1.5 ) #> Scale for colour is already present. #> Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.

title: statplot (development version)
text: table_basic: new function creating clean, formatted tables using tinytable. Supports column-level rounding control via named lists vectors, optional comma formatting large numbers, configurable handling missing values. default uses bold header rows can respect column label attributes.

title: statplot (development version)
text: plot_pvalue_barplot: default -log10(p) x-axis breaks now use meaningful p-value ticks (1, 0.2, 0.1, 0.01, 0.001) instead equally-spaced integer breaks; label formatting simplified remove "<" prefix previously shown smallest p-value. plot_pathways: default color_low, color_mid, color_high changed NULL "blue", "white", "red", respectively, providing sensible default diverging color palette without requiring explicit user specification. plot_pathways, plot_dotmap: new legend_position argument (default "right") allows users control legend position via ggplot2::theme(legend.position = ...). Common values include "right", "left", "top", "bottom", "none" hide legend. combine_pvalues: now allows p-values equal 1 (previously rejected); validation changed p > 0 & p < 1 p > 0 & p <= 1. plot_confidence_intervals: refactor — replace monolithic style parameter independent arguments visual encoding layout: color_col, color_values, shape_col, point_shapes (shape-based grouping), plus separator reference-line controls (show_separators, sep_linetype, sep_linewidth, sep_color, vline_xintercept, vline_linetype, vline_color) dodge_width. makes styling explicit composable simplifies legend handling. plot_confidence_intervals: ensure shape_col coerced factor stable, sorted level ordering; validate point_shapes length number shape levels provide clearer legend overrides color_col /shape_col used.

title: statplot (development version)
text: plot_covariate_heatmap: removed whitespace padding covariate bars plot borders applying expand = ggplot2::expansion(0) applicable scales vertical horizontal layouts.

title: statplot 0.5.0 - 2026-04-10
text: theme_bw2 theme_classic2: commonly used themes plot_confidence_intervals: added pvalue_plot_margin argument parameterize p-value panel plot margin (points), default c(5.5, 12, 5.5, 0). prevents x-axis labels appended p-value barplot clipped panel composed right using patchwork. hallmark_pathway_categories: new internal dataset mapping Hallmark pathway names (term column, HALLMARK_ prefix removed) biological process categories defined Table 1 Liberzon et al. (2015). dataset includes runtime validation step bundled hallmark_t2g data ensure pathway names align MSigDB. plot_covariate_heatmap: new function draw vertical covariate strips (one strip per covariate). Supports categorical covariates (named color vectors) . plot_barplot_by_group: new two-condition bar plot function comparing numeric outcome across groups. Supports error_direction = c("", ""), configurable bar_width, bar_gap, bar_padding, optional facet layout via facet/strip_position, custom bracket labels via label_col. plot_pathways: new pathway_cats pathway_cat_colors arguments let users fill pathway nodes biological process category. accompanying legend_pathway_fill_title legend_pathway_fill_dot_size arguments customise category fill legend, gene effect-size colour scale remains independent.

title: statplot 0.5.0 - 2026-04-10
text: plot_dot_whiskers renamed plot_confidence_intervals. function signature behavior significantly enhanced: Parameter renames clarity: x → effect_size, xmin → ci_low, xmax → ci_high, label_col → id effect_size, ci_low, ci_high, id now required positional arguments (previously optional defaults) color_col: optional column coloring segments points (can group-based id-based) color_values: optional named character vector custom colors combine_pvalue_method: method combining p-values group_col supplied (default "fisher") Enhanced p-value panel logic: group_col specified, p-values now automatically combined across groups id using specified method, eliminating need pre-computed combined p-values. group_col NULL, individual p-values displayed directly. Improved styling: Supports shape-based color-based group distinction, flexible color shape customization options plot_barplot_by_group: changed bracket_offset default 0.08 1.0 updated parameter documentation clarify now absolute distance data units (formerly fraction y range). plot_dotmap: added short example demonstrating only_show_top_sig (shows top N rows combined p-value barplot) updated combine_pvalue_method parameter documentation reference combine_pvalues method details. combine_pvalues: default methods changed '' 'CMC'; documentation tests updated reflect new default. plot_dotmap: default combine_pvalue_method now "fisher" (previously "CMC" supplied via match.arg()); documentation examples updated reflect new default choice. plot_pvalue_barplot: improved legend behaviour — vertical significance line legend requested (vline = TRUE, vline_legend = TRUE) colour scale now stores explicit named values vector code tests can programmatically find named alpha entry; also fill legend uses override.aes remove line linetype legend keys showing p/q bars legend keys render solid/filled swatches. plot_pvalue_barplot: added vline_legend optionally include suppress vertical significance line legend key. plot_pathways: default legend_pathway_size_title changed "Num. genes\n pathway" default legend_color_title changed "Gene effect size".

title: statplot 0.5.0 - 2026-04-10
text: plot_dotmap: combine_pvalues() now called methods = combine_pvalue_method instead subsetting result name. Previously, combine_pvalues() defaulted computing "CMC", requesting method (e.g. "fisher") returned NA, causing combined p-value barplot draw bars.

title: statplot 0.4.0 - 2026-03-26
text: plot_data_avail_by_group: tile plot showing availability (0/1) data types across groups, black border, whitespace padding, optional bold axis labels. plot_pathways: new legend_fixed_dot_size argument (numeric vector) fixes size-legend breaks limits user-supplied gene-count values multiple plots composed patchwork share identical visual size scale.
